message:
feat(scheduler): add O(1) LocalQueue fast path and non-stealable local task pinning

Introduce a layered work-scheduling strategy with an O(1) IntrusiveStack
fast path for spawn/wake, a non-stealable local_ready queue for !Send tasks,
and proper spawn_local API wiring through thread-local schedulers.

Scheduler changes (three_lane.rs):
- Add LocalQueue (IntrusiveStack) as O(1) fast path before PriorityScheduler
- Add local_ready queue for !Send tasks that must never be stolen
- Refactor spawn()/wake()/schedule_local_cancel() for layered scheduling:
  fast queue > PriorityScheduler > global injector
- Add ScopedLocalReady TLS guard and schedule_local_task()/
  schedule_on_current_local() helpers
- Update run_loop() to install all TLS guards
- Update try_ready_work() drain order: local_ready > fast_queue > global
- Update try_steal() to try fast stealers before PriorityScheduler heaps
  with debug_assert guards against stealing local tasks
- Update wakers to route through local_ready
- Add ~550 lines of tests: fast-path preference, steal behavior,
  local-task isolation, concurrency correctness, waker fallback paths

Supporting changes:
- scope.rs: Simplify spawn_local() to return TaskHandle only (not tuple),
  wire to real local scheduler via schedule_on_current_local()
- task.rs: Add is_local field to TaskRecord with mark_local()/is_local()
- priority.rs: Add cancel-lane promotion parity regression tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
